title: Ceratozamia tenuis
taxonomy: Ceratozamia tenuis is a species of cycad within the family Zamiaceae. It belongs to the order Cycadales. The genus Ceratozamia is classified under the class Pinopsida and the phylum Coniferophyta. As a member of the Zamiaceae family, Ceratozamia tenuis is related to other cycad species such as Ceratozamia fischeriana. This classification indicates its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Ceratozamia tenuis is a species of cycad, a type of ancient, seed-producing plant with a woody trunk and palmate leaves. Traditionally, Ceratozamia tenuis has been used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as cycasin, a toxic compound, as well as other glycosides and flavonoids, which may contribute to its medicinal properties. Ceratozamia tenuis is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures, which are marketed for their anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects.
